examples: The teams competed to see who could collect (the) most money.
I spent most time in Rome because most of Venice is flooded.

definitions.text: superlative degree of many: the comparatively largest number of (construed with the definite article)
examples: The team with the most points wins.

definitions.text: superlative degree of many: the majority of; more than half of (construed without the definite article)
examples: Most bakers and dairy farmers have to get up early.
Winning was not important for most participants.

definitions.text: Forms the superlative of many adjectives.
examples: This is the most important example.

definitions.text: To a great extent or degree; highly; very.
examples: This is a most unusual specimen.

definitions.text: The greater part of a group, especially a group of people.
examples: Most want the best for their children.
The peach was juicier and more flavourful than most.
The most I can offer for the house is $150,000.
Most of the penguins were friendly and curious.
Most of the rice was spoiled.
